commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes agent auth-label resolution so custom provider `models.json` keys are reported before environment fallbacks, with regression coverage for the `zai-light` shared-base-url case.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +101. Total +104 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `resolveModelAuthLabel` and `resolveApiKeyForProvider` use separate precedence paths, and the linked issue narrows the symptom to status display. I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Queue a focused repair because the blocking issue is a narrow resolver/test precedence correction on the existing PR branch.

target.title: fix(agents): align custom provider auth labels with runtime (#82020)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `resolveModelAuthLabel` so `/status` and other UI surfaces show the same auth source as runtime resolution for custom providers like `zai-light`.
- Handle per-entry literal `apiKey` values and prefer `models.json` credentials before env fallbacks (e.g. shared `ZAI_API_KEY`), matching `resolveApiKeyForProvider` precedence.

Closes #82020

## Real behavior proof

With `zai` and `zai-light` sharing the same base URL, a literal `zai-light` key in config, and `ZAI_API_KEY` set in the environment:

```powershell
pnpm exec tsx -e "(async()=>{const { resolveModelAuthLabel } = await import('./src/agents/model-auth-label.ts'); const { resolveApiKeyForProvider } = await import('./src/agents/model-auth.ts'); const cfg={models:{providers:{zai:{api:'openai-completions',baseUrl:'https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/coding/paas/v4',apiKey:'zai-api-key',models:[]},'zai-light':{api:'openai-completions',baseUrl:'https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/coding/paas/v4',apiKey:'sk-light-only',models:[{id:'glm-5-turbo'}]}}}}; process.env.ZAI_API_KEY='zai-env-key'; const auth=await resolveApiKeyForProvider({provider:'zai-light',cfg}); const label=resolveModelAuthLabel({provider:'zai-light',cfg}); console.log(JSON.stringify({authSource:auth.source,authKey:auth.apiKey,label},null,2));})();"
```

Output:

```json
{
  "authSource": "models.json",
  "authKey": "sk-light-only",
  "label": "api-key (models.json)"
}
```

Before this change, the label path could report `api-key (env: ZAI_API_KEY)` even though runtime auth already used the `zai-light` literal key from `models.json`.

## Tests

- `pnpm test src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts -t "82020|zai-light"`

## Test plan

- [x] Scoped Vitest regression for `zai-light` + shared `ZAI_API_KEY`
- [x] Terminal proof that runtime key and status label agree
- [ ] Maintainer: Telegram `/status` on a config with `zai` + `zai-light` shows `api-key (models.json)` for the custom provider
target.author: ly85206559
